risky_changes.proposed: About the Role In this role, you will help accelerate the adoption of utility EV managed charging and virtual power plant programs by shaping the regulatory, policy, and market conditions that enable WeaveGrid’s growth. The ideal candidate is a hybrid policy strategist and market builder: someone who can shape markets externally, build coalitions, manage consultants, brief internal teams, and turn regulatory developments into commercial insight. You will work closely with Business Development, ClientSuccess, Product, Marketing, Legal, and executive leadership to ensure WeaveGrid is positioned to win in priority markets and help utilities unlock the full grid value of EVs and other distributed energy resources. Responsibilities Primary responsibilities include: Regulatory Strategy & Market Development - Develop and execute WeaveGrid’s regulatory and policy engagement strategy across priority North American markets. - Identify, monitor, and prioritize high-leverage regulatory proceedings, legislative developments, utility planning processes, and market design initiatives relevant to transportation electrification, managed charging, VPPs, DER flexibility, rate design, distribution planning, DSM, and grid modernization. - Translate regulatory and policy developments into actionable recommendations for WeaveGrid’s commercial strategy, client engagement, product roadmap, and market prioritization. - Support market entry and expansion strategies by identifying policy tailwinds, regulatory barriers, utility program opportunities, and potential coalition partners. - Support utility program approvals and expansions by helping clients and internal teams navigate regulatory processes. - Identify new business models, program designs, and partnership opportunities emerging from federal, state, and utility-level policy initiatives. Regulatory Engagement, Advocacy & Thought Leadership - Draft, review, and coordinate regulatory comments, testimony, white papers, policy briefs, and other advocacy materials. - Represent WeaveGrid in regulatory proceedings, stakeholder workshops, industry convenings, and trade associations while building relationships with regulators, policymakers, utilities, advocates, NGOs, and other stakeholders. - Partner with Marketing to create thought leadership materials, conference content, case studies, and other public-facing narratives that strengthen WeaveGrid’s market position. Coalition Building & Consultant Management - Identify opportunities for WeaveGrid to work through coalitions, trade associations, industry groups, and aligned partners to advance shared policy objectives. - Cultivate and manage outside consultants, attorneys, lobbyists, and policy advisors to support market monitoring, regulatory filings, stakeholder engagement, and advocacy strategy. Internal Intelligence & Cross-Functional Enablement - Serve as WeaveGrid’s internal expert on regulatory and policy developments affecting priority markets, customers, and commercial opportunities. - Brief Business Development, Client Success, Product, Marketing, Legal, and executive stakeholders on relevant policy developments, market signals, and implications for WeaveGrid. - Create repeatable internal processes for tracking proceedings, summarizing developments, flagging risks and opportunities, and enabling commercial teams to act on regulatory intelligence. - Partner with Business Development teams to support RFPs, proposals, bid defenses, customer conversations, account plan updates, and new market development. - Partner with Client Success teams to support existing utility clients navigating regulatory approvals, filings, program design changes, evaluation requirements, or stakeholder engagement. - Partner with Product and Solutions teams to ensure regulatory trends, utility planning needs, and market requirements inform product strategy and roadmap decisions. About You - You are a strategic policy and market development leader who understands how regulatory decisions shape commercial opportunities. - You are comfortable operating at multiple altitudes: setting strategy, writing comments, managing consultants, briefing executives, supporting customer conversations, and building external relationships. - You have strong judgment about where to engage, when to influence directly, when to work through coalitions, and how to focus limited startup resources on the highest-leverage opportunities. - You are an excellent communicator who can translate complex regulatory, technical, and market developments into clear implications for commercial, product, and executive audiences. - You are excited by the opportunity to help shape the future of transportation electrification, grid flexibility, managed charging, and virtual power plants. - You embrace WeaveGrid’s organizational values of Accountability, Collaboration, Focus, Urgency, Empathy, and Learning. Requirements - 8+ years of full-time experience in some combination of energy policy, utility regulation, market development, public affairs, clean energy advocacy, regulatory strategy, management consulting, utility programs, or related fields. - Strong understanding of electric utility regulation and one or more of the following areas: transportation electrification, distributed energy resources, demand response, DSM, VPPs, rate design, grid modernization, distribution planning, IRPs, Distribution System Plans, or utility program design. - Track record of achieving policy outcomes by engaging with public utility commissions, state energy offices, political leaders, utilities, trade associations, consumer advocates, NGOs, or other energy policy stakeholders, with the judgment and credibility to represent a company externally. - Excellent writing skills, including experience drafting or contributing to regulatory comments, policy briefs, testimony, white papers, public presentations, or executive briefings. - Ability to translate policy and regulatory developments into actionable insights for commercial, customer success, product, and executive teams. - Demonstrated ability to manage multiple proceedings, markets, projects, and stakeholders simultaneously with limited supervision. - Experience managing outside consultants, attorneys, advisors, coalitions, or trade association engagement. - Based in the United States and willing to travel periodically for customer meetings, regulatory convenings, conferences, and internal team events. Preferred Qualifications - Experience in transportation electrification, EV managed charging, DERMS, VPPs, demand flexibility, utility program design, or grid-edge software. - Experience supporting utility program approvals, rate cases, IRPs, distribution system planning, DSM filings, transportation electrification plans, or VPP/demand flexibility proceedings. - Established network in the energy regulatory, utility, transportation electrification, or DER policy community. - Advanced degree in public policy, law, business, energy systems, engineering, or a related field preferred. Compensation The total compensation for this opportunity includes a base salary range of $138,600 - 198,000 plus equity and benefits. This is our target compensation range and is subject to multiple factors including role, level, experience, and location. Please note that WeaveGrid may not necessarily hire individuals at or near the top of the base salary range. As you go through our interview process, our recruiter will work with you to identify a competitive base salary within the proposed range and combine it with an equity package to get you excited about your future at WeaveGrid. San Francisco Bay Area preferred, remote considered. risky_changes.title: Quantitative Analyst
risky_changes.current:
Position Specific Description:

Lead the development, implementation, and maintenance of financial, statistical, and commodity-focused models, templates, programs, and processes for the Valuation team. Relies on knowledge of algebra, statistics, probability, present value, portfolio theory, risk and return, discounted cash flows, comparable analysis, energy markets, logic, binomial tree modeling, and calculus. Leverages knowledge of coding, information systems, total quality management, and project management to effectively and efficiently deliver a system of valuation.

Deliver sound financial valuation of wholesale power derivatives, including load following, deterministic, and block energy products, mostly bundled with capacity, renewable energy credits, ancillary services, and other products to serve residential, commercial, industrial, and municipal customers. Executes valuation process for transactions ranging from approximately 1MW to 1000MW with 3 to 36 month terms that yield revenues up to $700MM. Values transactions by evaluating power futures or fundamental commodity estimates to create forecasts based on historical data analysis, multiple regression, number theory, logic, Monte Carlo simulations, scenario analysis, and proprietary models.

Executes regional forecasting assignments for energy, volatility, capacity, renewable energy credits and ancillary services. Researches laws, regulations, tariffs, and market procedures in a variety of markets to create models and forecast applicable costs. May complete ad hoc analysis of forwards, futures, options, interest rates, market conditions, exotic structures, weather products, and other financial or commercial assignments required to achieve group objectives.

risky_changes.proposed: Who We Are CleanCapital is a leading independent power producer focused on distributed clean energy, including middle-market solar and energy storage. The company develops, builds, owns, operates, and invests in clean energy assets across the U.S. Leveraging over a decade of clean energy expertise and over $1.5 billion in investments, CleanCapital has built a diverse portfolio of operating assets and a growing project pipeline. By combining institutional capital with in-house expertise and regional development partnerships, CleanCapital delivers economically sound projects that drive more clean megawatts to the grid, solidifying energy dominance in the U.S. Location of Position If the candidate is based in the NYC metropolitan area, there is an in-office requirement of a minimum of 2 days per week. Candidates located outside of the NYC metropolitan area will be fully remote, except for work-related travel. What We Offer - Opportunity to join a top-notch, collaborative team of professionals - Fantastic team environment and collaborative culture - Professional development opportunities to grow into an industry leader - 100% paid for single and family benefits plus 401k matching program - The range for this position is ($102,000 - $138,000) plus an annual performance-based bonus Our Commitment to Diversity CleanCapital is committed to fostering a diverse and equitable workforce reflective of our community while also valuing each persons’ unique perspective, skills, and experiences. CleanCapital does not discriminate against any individual’s race, religion, sexual orientation,. Position Summary The Technical Asset Manager will monitor and manage assigned distributed generation projects across the CleanCapital portfolio to ensure optimal generation performance of the company’s solar photovoltaic (PV) and battery energy storage systems. Overall responsibilities will include project reporting, performance data analysis, preventive and corrective maintenance programming and oversight, preparation of capital and O&M budgets and collaborating with the internal ﬁnancial group to support reporting requirements. The Technical Asset Manager will work closely with and manage a number of outsourced Solar O&M providers to achieve overall portfolio performance goals. The ideal candidate will also be an AI-curious, forward-thinking individual who actively integrates AI tools into their daily professional routine. Core Responsibilities - Ensure solar and battery portfolio is performing at or above forecasted production. - Maximize the total returns for each asset through the analysis of system efficiency and identifying opportunities for improved performance. - Review performance reports and project speciﬁc performance data; Investigate issues and initiate actions to improve performance. - Develop and manage project budgets, models, contracts, and stakeholder and customer relations. - Work closely with numerous departments including Accounting / Financial Planning & Analysis (FP&A), outside service providers, o takers (contract counterparty or customers) and partners. - Review and analyze issues with current portfolio ensuring regulatory, interconnection and Health, Safety, Security and Environment compliance; Ensure assets are operated and managed consistent with CleanCapital HSSE Policy. - Assist Accounting in preparing investor reports, budgets for investors and lenders and resolve any issues or questions. - Collaborate with FP&A and Accounting to ensure accuracy of billing to customers and resolve issues if needed. - Review, track and approve expenses incurred by assets and their agents, including external and internal charges. - Manage relationships with stakeholders in solar projects e.g., property owners. - Provide timely work products to support management and reporting of operating assets. - Contribute to the development of policies and procedures to ensure all procedural, legislative and contract requirements are fulﬁlled. - Participate in the development of an asset compliance calendar. Where speciﬁed, manage and monitor compliance in all contracts and activities. - Identify, assess, and support execution of Asset Optimization initiatives by preparing cost/beneﬁt analysis for each optimization initiative, considering the terms of the contracts associated with the asset, asset performance, engineering cost estimates and implementation schedules from the various subject matter experts. Ensure to ﬂesh out the true value of each identiﬁed initiative and package into a commercial value proposition for review and approval by senior management. - Job description may evolve over time. - Occasional travel expected. Education Requirements - Bachelor’s Degree required. Degree in engineering or any other technical ﬁeld - additional education in ﬁnance or accounting preferred Technical Skills and Experience - 3+ years of experience required in the following areas: - Energy storage and solar Operations - Technical understanding and experience with solar generation and energy storage; Experience working with computerized DAS/SCADA/Monitoring systems, interconnection agreements and data management. - Negotiating contracts relating to Operations and Maintenance and corrective maintenance costs, warranty claims, etc. - Distributed generation solar PV and energy storage asset management – North America - Managing a portfolio of energy storage solar sites - Experience working in a high performing multi-disciplinary team. - Proven AI literacy, including understanding AI strengths, limitations, and ethical considerations. - Preferred experience with, but not required: - - PV and BESS operations and maintenance (O&M) - PV system repowerGreen ﬁeld and/or brownﬁeld development is an asset. - Budget preparation and management. risky_changes.title: Reliability Coordinator
risky_changes.current: We’re seeking a Reliability Coordinator to manage real-time issue response, dispatch coordination, and technical troubleshooting across our customers’ EV charging portfolios. You will work across Zendesk, work orders, CSMS/OCPP data, customer communications, OEM guidance, internal notes, and EMPWR to coordinate service and support reliable charger operations. The ideal candidate is detail-oriented, technically curious, comfortable working through ambiguity, and able to translate fragmented information into clear steps for technicians, electricians, OEMs, partners, and customers. Coordinate real-time response to EV charging station issues across assigned customer portfolios. Dispatch ChargerHelp technicians and third-party service partners with clear, actionable instructions You will assign technicians, manage 3rd-party service partners, track SLA performance, and ensure customers receive timely, high-quality service
risky_changes.proposed: We’re seeking a Reliability Coordinator to manage real-time issue response, dispatch coordination, and technical troubleshooting across our customers’ EV charging portfolios. You will work across Zendesk, work orders, CSMS/OCPP data, customer communications, OEM guidance, internal notes, and EMPWR to coordinate service and support reliable charger operations. The ideal candidate is detail-oriented, technically curious, comfortable working through ambiguity, and able to translate fragmented information into clear steps for technicians, electricians, OEMs, partners, and customers. What You’ll Do - Coordinate real-time response to EV charging station issues across assigned customer portfolios - Dispatch ChargerHelp technicians and third-party service partners with clear, actionable instructions - Review Zendesk tickets, customer communications, work orders, CSMS/OCPP data, internal notes, photos, and OEM guidance to understand reported issues - Help determine whether issues appear related to hardware, software, network Develop working knowledge of how issues present across hardware, software, network connectivity, configuration, electrical conditions, vehicle-side behavior, and customer process. Review completed work orders and tickets to spot recurring failure patterns, workflow gaps, and opportunities to eliminate unnecessary truck rolls - Prepare customer reports, QBR inputs, and operational summaries using EMPWR and related systems - Support customer onboarding and periodic account check-ins - Contribute feedback to improve EMPWR workflows, diagnostic tools, service playbooks, and reliability processes - Hands-on experience in dispatching, field service coordination, technical support, real-time operations in EV charging infrastructure or similar energy distribution assets - Strong problem-solving skills and the ability to work through unclear, incomplete, or conflicting information - Technical curiosity and persistence; you leverage EMPWR to its fullest, and when the system raises a question you cannot answer, you go find it through fault codes, manuals, prior tickets, and partner guidance - Ability to distinguish verified facts from assumptions, reported symptoms, and unresolved questions - Comfort working with operational software, dashboards, spreadsheets, ticketing systems, exported data, and internal platforms - Ability to translate complex or incomplete technical information into clear instructions for technicians, electricians, OEMs, partners, and customers - Strong written and verbal communication skills - Strong attention to detail and disciplined documentation habits - Customer-first mindset with the ability to communicate calmly and clearly during escalations - Comfort working in a fast-paced, evolving environment where processes may still be developing - Ability to leverage data and performance metrics to drive informed operational decisions - Familiarity with EV charging hardware, EVSE operations, OCPP, CSMS platforms, Zendesk, Slack, or field service tools is strongly preferred Success Looks Like - Technicians are dispatched with clear instructions, the right , and the right tools - Work orders are complete, accurate, and useful for future troubleshooting - Customers receive timely, clear, and credible updates - Repeat issues are identified and escalated before they become larger problems - Ambiguous charger failures are moved toward resolution through structured investigation - The team reduces unnecessary truck rolls by improving diagnosis before dispatch - EMPWR becomes more accurate through better documentation, data capture, and coordinator feedback - Internal teams have better visibility into recurring issues, customer risks, and service gaps This Role Is a Fit If You - Like solving operational puzzles - Are comfortable digging through messy information to find the signal - Do not stop at “the ticket says it is broken” - Can ask better questions when the root cause is unclear - Care about documentation because you know future troubleshooting depends on it - Can coordinate calmly across customers, technicians, OEMs, software partners, and internal teams - Are interested in EV charging infrastructure, reliability, and the future of field service Why This Role Matters EV charging reliability is not always straightforward.

risky_changes.current: In this position, the Digital Advertising Associate will work to advance the programmatic and strategic growth of GoodPower through implementing, managing, and improving GoodPower’s paid advertising campaign strategies in key states and nationally. They will work to strengthen GoodPower’s role and impact in advocating for an end to the era of fossil fuels, a swift and equitable transition to renewable energy, and an increase in diverse youth participation in our democracy as well as devise creative strategies to inoculate persuadable audiences for GoodPower’s current and future white label entities
risky_changes.proposed: Remote-based in the US Overview GoodPower works globally to unlock the enormous economic potential of the energy transition—more affordable energy bills, better and more abundant jobs, healthier food, economic security for families and farmers, and a better economy that works for all of us. We operate at the intersection of digital media, smart tech, civic participation, and advocacy to reach millions of people annually with our work to: lower costs and create jobs, shift culture to transform beliefs and behaviors, and accelerate the deployment of decarbonized technologies like: renewables, regenerative agriculture and electric vehicles—one individual, one neighborhood, one community at a time. GoodPower is at an exciting, pivotal moment as we launch our new strategic plan through 2030 to level up all areas of our work–growing our organization 5x over the five years and relentlessly honing our skills and expertise to be the best that we can be to transform our renewable energy economy. Job Summary In this position, the Digital Advertising Associate will work to advance the programmatic and strategic growth of GoodPower through implementing, managing, and improving GoodPower’s paid advertising campaign strategies in key states and nationally. They will work to strengthen GoodPower’s role and impact in advocating for an end to the era of fossil fuels, a swift and equitable transition to renewable energy, and an increase in diverse youth participation in our democracy as well as devise creative strategies to inoculate persuadable audiences for GoodPower’s current and future white label entities. This is a future-facing role for an ads expert who thrives at the convergence of digital innovation, audience segmentation, and data-driven campaign performance. This is a full-time, remote position based in the United States. The Digital Advertising Associate will work from home, meet regularly by video chat with GoodPower team members and partners, and conduct the majority of activities both online and in-person, when safe. The Digital Advertising Associate must be available for travel within the United States to participate in staff retreats and/or conferences. Key Responsibilities - Campaign Strategy & Management - Design, launch, and manage digital and traditional advertising campaigns across multiple platforms (Meta, Google Ads, YouTube, X,, Reddit, Twitch, audio, programmatic DSPs, and terrestrial media). - Develop budgets and paid media plans; monitor performance to ensure delivery of key metrics. - Manage audiences, targeting, creative assets, and tracking systems to optimize performance. - Creative Development & Optimization - Write and refine ad copy; collaborate with vendors and staff to produce engaging creatives. - Build and optimize action pages to drive list growth, conversions, and lower cost-per-lead. - Leverage retargeting and segmentation to increase retention and campaign effectiveness. - Implement new tracking and reporting methods to enhance performance insights. - Cross-Departmental Collaboration - Partner with program and campaign teams to align advertising with organizational priorities (e.g., voter registration, siting & permitting, clean energy acceleration, IRA defense, workforce development, and white-label initiatives). - Support the development team with funder collateral, including campaign impact reports and presentations. - Coordinate with communications and compliance teams to ensure proper C3/C4 activity reporting. - Data, Insights & Innovation - Monitor and analyze KPIs; provide actionable recommendations for optimization. - Research and test innovative strategies to expand reach, engagement, and efficiency. - Stay current on audience sentiment, platform innovations, and industry trends Qualifications Minimum Qualifications - 3 plus years of experience in the digital marketing or digital organizing field - Strong analytical skills with the ability to set priorities, and complete work and meet deadlines with minimal supervision - Strong Excel or Google Sheets skills - Self-starter with strong organizational skills and creativity and can take big-picture strategy and couple it with strategic implementation. - Familiarity with digital tools (eg. GSuite, Slack, Canva, Jira, etc.) Desired Qualifications - Experience building, managing, and evaluating paid advertising campaigns within Ads Manager and Google Ads - Experience working with Google Tag Manager, Pixel, and other event-tracking mechanisms - Copywriting experience - Graphic design experience This description reflects GoodPower’s assignment of essential functions; it does not restrict the tasks that may be assigned. GoodPower retains the right to change or assign other duties to this position at any time. Employees must be able to perform the essential functions of the position satisfactorily. Reasonable accommodations will be made to enable employees with disabilities to perform the essential functions of their job, absent undue hardship. GoodPower has a zero-tolerance standard for abuse and inappropriate behavior by staff members. Job Summary Position Details - Annual salary range: $70,000-80,000, commensurate with experience - Generous benefits include: Medical, Dental, Vision, 403b retirement savings plan, Vacation, Sabbatical, Paid Parental Leave, 2 Floating Holidays, 2 Community Service Floating Holidays, sick time, two weeks of full-staff time off (July 4 week and Christmas-New Years week) and 13 observed holidays - GoodPower is an equal-opportunity employer that highly values staff diversity - Remote Application Guidelines Apply Here GoodPower is an equal-opportunity employer that highly values diversity, equity, and inclusion and views the climate crisis as a social justice crisis. People who identify as Black, Indigenous, and People of Color (BIPOC), people who experience gender oppression, people with disabilities, and people who identify as members of the LGBTQIA+ community are particularly encouraged to apply. risky_changes.proposed: About Us ChargerHelp! Inc is a California, USA-based technology company that has developed a mobile application and web-based platform for on-demand repair of electric vehicle charging stations. risky_changes.proposed: About Us ChargerHelp! Inc is a California, USA-based technology company that has developed a mobile application and web-based platform for on-demand repair of electric vehicle charging stations. We are committed to professional and workforce development, diversity and inclusion, leveraging technology to remove barriers, and enabling economic mobility in underserved communities. We have a social responsibility to reduce our carbon footprint and lower greenhouse emissions. ChargerHelp! is a driving force in the Cleantech space, executing a forward thinking, progressive approach to workforce development and EVSE field service. We develop the technology and provide the education and training to support growing demands and evolving trends in the electrical vehicle industry. Role Overview We are seeking a Certified EVSE Technician to join our local workforce in maintaining the critical infrastructure of the electric vehicle revolution. In this role, you will be the face of ChargerHelp! in the field, responsible for inspecting, troubleshooting, and repairing EV charging stations to ensure they are clean, functional, and performing within design specifications. As a Certified CH! Technician, you will utilize our proprietary mobile application to document site reviews, perform preventative maintenance, and execute complex break/fix services. The ideal candidate is a self-starter with a strong technical aptitude who is eager to operate at the intersection of electrical hardware and software diagnostics. What You’ll Do - Inspect, troubleshoot, and service electrical charging infrastructure using a range of tools, from computer diagnostics to basic electrical equipment. - Measure charging infrastructure performance using appropriate technical tools and complete "driver experience" inspections via the CH! mobile app. - Maintain detailed, real-time documentation of all repairs, software troubleshooting, and site status updates to ensure project transparency. - Ensure all assigned services are delivered on time and within scope; escalate technical or site-level risks to management as needed. - Represent ChargerHelp! at client locations with the highest standards of professional conduct and communication. - Follow strict safety protocols to minimize project risks and ensure the safety of the technician and the public. - Attend industry conferences and mandatory CH! training sessions to maintain certification and stay current on evolving EVSE technology. What You Bring - Strong hands-on experience in electrical, mechanical, or related field service; experience with electrical troubleshooting is highly preferred. - An enterprising attitude with the ability to operate independently with limited supervision in a fast-paced startup environment. - Excellent client-facing skills and the ability to communicate effectively with internal operations and management teams. - Resourceful and diligent documentation habits to ensure work orders are accurate and comprehensive. - Ability to lift, bend, climb, and work in various environments, including ladders, extreme temperatures (heat/cold), and confined spaces. - A valid driver’s license and a good driving record demonstrating the ability to safely operate a motor vehicle. - High school diploma or equivalent; general knowledge of automotive or vehicle systems is a plus. Licensing & Certifications - Preferred: OSHA 10/30 certification, NFPA 70E, or equivalent safety training. - Mandatory: Valid driver’s license and clean driving record. What’s Essential - High School Diploma or equivalent. - Valid Driver's License with clean driving record. - Reliable transportation. - Basic computer skills for diagnostics, reporting, and communication. - Demonstrable electrical experience in commercial or industrial settings. - Minimum 3 - 5 years of field technician experience as a licensed electrician. - Completion of ChargerHelp! Training upon onboarding. Physical Requirements - Physical and Environmental Demands include but not limited to the following: lifting, bending, climbing, kneeling, reaching, sitting, standing, working from ladders, walking, working in extreme temperatures (cold/heat), confined quarters, excessive noise and high voltage - Travel required up to 75%

risky_changes.proposed: Communications Director Status: Full-time, Exempt Salary Range (annual): $85,000 - $110,000 Schedule: 40 hours per week, Monday-Friday, 8 a.m.- 5 p.m. Pacific Time Veloz is based in Sacramento, California. This is a 100% remote work position in the U.S. with a prioritization given to candidates based on the West Coast. Travel within and/or to California and nationwide is expected up to 5% annually. About Veloz Veloz is a national nonpartisan (c)(3) nonprofit with a vision of the future where transportation is electric for all. We drive toward that all-electric future through strategic communications, unprecedented collaborations and purposeful convening. Veloz is the power behind the nation’s largest and most inventive multi-partner education campaign for electric vehicles, the architect of events and programming garnering national attention, and the organization bringing together a high-powered, diverse Board of Directors, Public Policy Board and members from the public and private sectors. Opportunity and Position Description In this position, the Content Marketing Manager is part of a passionate and collaborative organization that is changing the conversation about EVs nationwide by sparking a virtuous cycle of consumer awareness and demand. Veloz plays a unique and important role in the national EV landscape, and now is the time to take our efforts to an exponentially more impactful level. This requires a clear and inspiring vision, sophisticated strategies, significant and sustained financial support, and smart implementation. Reporting to the Communications Director and partnering with the Veloz team, the Content Marketing Manager leads Veloz’s industry-facing and consumer facing social, newsletter and digital content across several platforms. This focus may require the ability to develop design assets. This individual plays a critical role in making the electric for all vision a reality by executing aspects of a comprehensive communications and marketing strategy to raise awareness of Veloz and Electric For All, developing and delivering high-quality and high-value content and working alongside the Communications team to manage aspects of our industry-focused and consumer-facing communications efforts. In addition, this individual will provide a supporting role to communications leadership on -day communications activities Job Duties Social Media Marketing - Develop and maintain a social media calendar with strategic content, posts and analytics. - Post content, manage and maintain all social media profiles for Veloz and Electric For All, growing our online presence and influence. - When applicable, Work with Veloz’s agency contractors to develop organic social support. - Manage and produce user generated content, and community management for both Veloz and Electric For All social channels - Actively participate in Veloz and Electric For All events by attending and developing social media activities and post-event messaging. Content Creation & Marketing - Create and deploy strong written and visual communications to generate timely content and updates for newsletters, both websites, social media, reports, and other materials distributed across Veloz and Electric For All marketing channels — including layout/design elements and A/B testing. - Provide event support via communications channels including, but not limited to digital asset creation, updating events on Veloz.org, and creating social media posts and graphics. - Research and implement content marketing and social media strategies to increase our reach and impact, while being responsive and timely to trends, current, member messaging and developments with social media posts. - Develop and produce an inventory of visual assets, including photos, videos and branding. - Ensure that the Veloz and Electric For All brands/tone are consistent across all media. Audience Growth & Engagement - Work with the communications team on a data-informed strategy to grow the Veloz and Electric For All social media channels and newsletters, while increasing website traffic and conversions on Veloz.org and ElectricForAll.org. - Manage, maintain a record of engagement and growth across all social media platforms for Veloz and Electric For All. - Manage and update Veloz and Electric for All email database including press, members, business development leads, event attendees, etc. - Analyze and present metrics for Veloz and Electric For All newsletters, social media channels and website to the team to help ensure a continuous virtuous cycle between our programs and our marketing efforts. - Engage with contact form outreach from both ElectricForAll.org and Veloz.org by responding to questions, recommending Veloz’s tools and services, and pointing people toward resources. Additional Support - Provide -day support to the communications leads. - Be available to support presentations and other materials supporting member engagement as needed. - Manage social media and design-related vendor contracts and process invoices as needed. - Be available to participate in key industry conferences, workshops and seminars, creating assets and activations on behalf of Veloz when appropriate. - Promote a culture of collaboration, communication, creativity with high performance standards along with, continual learning and a commitment to excellence. - Support the team in creating and ordering Veloz swag as needed for in-person events and other Veloz needs. Qualifications The Content Marketing Manager leads with their belief in and commitment to Veloz’s vision, mission and theory of change. The manager must champion state and federal carbon emissions reduction, public health and economic goals. Additionally, this person must demand the boldest possible ambition, innovation and collaboration of partners across sectors who are driving toward scalable transportation solutions. The Content Marketing Manager elevates their work to our levels of impact through their track record and expertise. The Content Marketing Manager also possesses the following skills and qualities: Required Qualifications - Minimum 5 years of experience in marketing, communications, social media management, digital content, or a related field. - Bachelor's degree in communications, marketing, website management, or a related field (or equivalent professional experience). - Proven track record of creating and executing content creation and marketing strategies that grow brand awareness and audience engagement. - Strong written communication skills, including experience with email marketing, website content, social media content, and other marketing materials. - Understanding of social media platforms (, X, Instagram,, TikTok) and how to tailor content to different audiences. - Proven project management skills, including timeline management, content planning, vendor coordination, and execution of marketing initiatives. - Highly collaborative work style with the ability to partner across teams and operationalize strategy. - Strong organizational skills, attention to detail, customer service orientation, and ability to manage multiple priorities. - Technologically savvy with experience using digital platforms to support marketing and communications efforts. - Strong customer service, attention to detail, organizational skills, and the ability to prioritize and execute. - Self-driven ambition, intellectual curiosity, and commitment to representing Veloz and its values. Preferred Qualifications - Knowledge of the electric vehicle industry (or related sectors such as clean transportation, sustainability, or clean energy). - Mastery of relationship-building skills with external stakeholders including public agencies, government regulators, Fortune 500 companies, startups, and nonprofits. - Proficiency with WordPress, Framer, Outlook, Zoom, Dropbox, Microsoft Office Suite, Mac OS, Google Analytics, Mailchimp, Sprout Social, and Slack. - Familiarity with Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG). - Experience with Adobe Creative Cloud or Canva. Compensation and Benefits The annual salary range for this position is commensurate with experience. Veloz offers a generous and competitive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, a 401k plan, life insurance, employee assistance programs, a wellness program, a monthly mobile phone/internet stipend for remote employees and more. In addition, Veloz offers paid leave plans which include personal, sick, and vacation time, along with 12 paid holidays. Our Commitment to Diversity, Equity & Inclusion Veloz values diversity, equity and inclusion. Our membership and leadership reflect strongly held principles around diversity of perspective, background and sector. Diversity is the cornerstone of our credibility and effectiveness and is reflected in how we operate, including search and hiring processes, promotion, and professional development. We are committed to an inclusive and transparent recruitment process and recruit and hire without regard to race, national origin, religion, gender,. People of diverse backgrounds are strongly encouraged to apply. Don’t meet every single requirement? At Veloz we are dedicated to building a diverse, inclusive, and authentic workplace, ( about our organizational values here) so if you’re excited about this role but your experience doesn’t align perfectly with every qualification in the job description, we encourage you to apply anyway. You might just be the perfect candidate for this role!
